preamble: The transformation of voiceless bilabial stop [p] to the voiced alveolar affricate [dz] is a complex shift involving palatalization, affrication, and voicing.

examples.note: Instances where voiceless labials become voiced alveolar affricates through intermediate stages of palatalization and voicing.
In specific dialectal contexts, labial-to-affricate shifts with voicing occur.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
